entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A non-industrial grain legume that was the first orphan legume to have a draft genome sequence.

entities.name: DRAFT GENOME
entities.description: The initial sequence of the pigeonpea genome used for genomic resource analysis.

entities.name: ASHA (ICPL 87119)
entities.description: An elite pigeonpea genotype used to develop the draft genome.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which led a genome sequencing effort.

entities.name: ILLUMINA GENOME ANALYZER
entities.description: An NGS platform used by ICRISAT for genome sequencing.

entities.name: HISEQ 2000 NGS PLATFORM
entities.description: An NGS platform used by ICRISAT for genome sequencing.

entities.name: DE-NOVO GENOME ASSEMBLY
entities.description: A method used to assemble raw reads into scaffolds.

entities.name: SANGER-BASED BAC END SEQUENCES
entities.description: Sequences used in combination with de-novo assembly to assemble the genome.

entities.name: GENETIC MAP
entities.description: A resource used to assemble raw reads into scaffolds.

entities.name: TRANSPOSABLE ELEMENTS
entities.description: Repetitive DNA elements found in 49.61% of the assembled pigeonpea genome.

entities.name: NRCPB
entities.description: National Research Centre on Plant Biotechnology, which led a genome sequencing effort.

entities.name: 454 GS-FLX SEQUENCING CHEMISTRY
entities.description: Sequencing chemistry used by NRCPB to assemble sequence data.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: A trait associated with 1213 identified defense response genes.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: A trait associated with 152 identified genes.

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach facilitated by the available draft genome information.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of improving food availability in developing countries through targeted trait improvement.
